records.ety: [The same word as shade. See Shade.]

defs: A slight or temporary structure built to shade or shelter something; a structure often open in front; an outbuilding; a hut; as, a wagon shed; a wood shed.
quotes: The first Aletes born in lowly shed.
Sheds of reeds which summer's heat repel.

defs: A covered structure for housing aircraft; a hangar.

records.ety: [OE. scheden, sch<?/den, to pour, to part, AS. scādan, scedan, to pert, to separate; akin to OS. sk<?/<?/an, OFries. sk<?/tha, G. scheiden, OHG. sceidan, Goth. skaidan, and probably to Lith. skëdu I part, separate, L. scindere to cleave, to split, Gr. <?/<?/<?/, Skr. chid, and perch. also to L. caedere to cut. √159. Cf. Chisel, Concise, Schism, Sheading, Sheath, Shide.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shed; p. pr. & vb. n. Shedding.]

defs: To part with; to throw off or give forth from one's self; to emit; to diffuse; to cause to emanate or flow; to pour forth or out; to spill; as, the sun sheds light; she shed tears; the clouds shed rain.
quotes: Did Romeo's hand shed Tybalt's blood?
Twice seven consenting years have shed Their utmost bounty on thy head.

defs: To let fall; to throw off, as a natural covering of hair, feathers, shell; to cast; as, fowls shed their feathers; serpents shed their skins; trees shed leaves.
To cause to flow off without penetrating; as, a tight roof, or covering of oiled cloth, sheeds water.
To sprinkle; to intersperse; to cover.
To divide, as the warp threads, so as to form a shed, or passageway, for the shuttle.
To fall in drops; to pour.
quotes: Such a rain down from the welkin shadde.

defs: To let fall the parts, as seeds or fruit; to throw off a covering or envelope.
quotes: White oats are apt to shed most as they lie, and black as they stand.

defs: A parting; a separation; a division.
quotes: They say also that the manner of making the shed of newwedded wives' hair with the iron head of a javelin came up then likewise.

defs: The act of shedding or spilling; -- used only in composition, as in bloodshed.
That which parts, divides, or sheds; -- used in composition, as in watershed.
The passageway between the threads of the warp through which the shuttle is thrown, having a sloping top and bottom made by raising and lowering the alternate threads.
